title: Bitcoin Production and HIVE Shares Decline despite BTC Price Records
text: HIVE Digital Technologies reported mixed results for February 2024. While the Vancouver-based miner expanded its cryptocurrency reserves, actual production fell short of the previous month's output. Monthly bitcoin mining decreased from 234.6 BTC in January to 200 BTC in February, representing a daily average drop from 7.6 to 6.9 BTC. Despite lower production, HIVE's bitcoin holdings grew 10% to reach 2,131 BTC by month's end, valued at approximately $143 million at the time. By early March, holdings had increased further to 2,159 BTC. HIVE integrated newer Bitmain S21 Antminers to boost efficiency and reduce production costs. CEO Aydin Kilic emphasized that these machines provided sustainable stability by significantly reducing the cost of Bitcoin production. The announcement contrasted sharply with broader bitcoin gains. While Bitcoin reached historic highs, HIVE shares fell 25% year-to-date on Nasdaq, mirroring struggles across the mining sector despite cryptocurrency's strong 2024 performance.
